name: GPT-5.4 mini
archDisplay: Undisclosed architecture (presumed dense transformer); cost-efficient mid-tier sibling of GPT-5.4, optimized for coding, computer use, and subagent workloads, running more than 2x faster than the flagship
note: Mid-tier sibling of GPT-5.4, launched Mar 17 2026 (12 days after the flagship/Pro) via OpenAI's own 'Introducing GPT-5.4 mini and nano' announcement as a distinct, separately-priced, separately-benchmarked model -- not a rename or repricing of GPT-5 mini. Runs >2x faster than GPT-5 mini and beats it on every published coding/tool/knowledge/vision benchmark; available in ChatGPT (Free/Go default, fallback for others), Codex, and the API.

results.notes: SWE-Bench Pro (Public), xhigh reasoning effort. Flagship scores 57.7%, GPT-5 mini scores 45.7% on the same table. Cross-confirmed verbatim by datacamp.com/blog/gpt-5-4-mini-nano (independent secondary source).
Terminal-Bench 2.0, xhigh reasoning effort. Cross-confirmed verbatim by datacamp.com/blog/gpt-5-4-mini-nano.
OSWorld-Verified, xhigh reasoning effort. Cross-confirmed verbatim by datacamp.com/blog/gpt-5-4-mini-nano.
Humanity's Last Exam, no tools, xhigh reasoning effort (with-tools variant scores 41.5%).
MMMU-Pro (standard, no Python tool; the 'w/ Python' variant scores 78.0%), xhigh reasoning effort.
ARC-AGI-2 from Epoch AI's independent evaluation of GPT-5.4 mini, relayed via themodelbeat.com.
FrontierMath (Tiers 1-3) from Epoch AI's independent evaluation of GPT-5.4 mini, relayed via themodelbeat.com.
FrontierMath Tier 4 (hardest tier) from Epoch AI's independent evaluation of GPT-5.4 mini, relayed via themodelbeat.com.

AIME 2024/2025 (combined) from Epoch AI's independent evaluation of GPT-5.4 mini, relayed via themodelbeat.com. Note: a separate low-quality source (layerlens.ai) reported an implausible AIME figure of 6.67% for this model (lower than its own HLE score, and wildly inconsistent with peer models) -- that figure was checked and rejected as unreliable rather than used here.

verificationNotes: Existence and distinctness from GPT-5 mini confirmed by directly fetching OpenAI's own official launch post (openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-4-mini-and-nano/, dated Mar 17 2026), which explicitly benchmarks GPT-5.4 mini against GPT-5 mini side by side and states it 'significantly improves over GPT-5 mini across coding, reasoning, multimodal understanding, and tool use.' Pricing ($0.75/$4.50 per M), 400K context, and 128K max output independently confirmed via OpenAI's live developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-5.4-mini model card (own snapshot dated gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17, matching the announcement date). Independent secondary source datacamp.com/blog/gpt-5-4-mini-nano (fetched directly) corroborates the same benchmark table verbatim and its FAQ states plainly: 'Is GPT-5.4 mini just a faster GPT-5 mini? No, it's both faster and significantly stronger on benchmarks... while keeping a 400k context window.' All 7 result rows taken from OpenAI's own multi-table benchmark breakdown (Coding/Tool-calling/Intelligence/MM-Vision-CUA sections); Toolathlon and MCP Atlas scores were also published but omitted here since no corpus benchmark slug matches them.
